title: poa glauca var. crocata
intro: Poa glauca var. crocata, also known as Blue Fescue Grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic actions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and treating wounds. Blue Fescue Grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
